Title: IRBuilder.if_else() and IRBuilder.if_then() don't seem to work - llvmlite
URL: https://numba.discourse.group/t/irbuilder-if-else-and-irbuilder-if-then-dont-seem-to-work/2267
Content: # IRBuilder.if\_else() and IRBuilder.if\_then() don't seem to work. Not sure if this belongs to `llvmlite` channel or the main `numba` channel, but it seems like `IRBuilder.if_then` and `IRBuilder.if_else` aren’t working. I am trying to write a very simple `numba` `intrinsic` function that will return `1` if the supplied value `x` is greater than `0` or return `1` otherwise. The `intrinsic` function looks like below code snippet, it’s called `check_value()`:. def codegen(context, builder, signature, args):. gtz = builder.icmp_signed(">", args[0], args[0].type(0)). a = context.get_constant(signature.args[0], 1). and the function to be `njit`ted looks like this:. and this is how it’s been used:. and when I run it, I am getting this output:. How do I make it work? As the name implies, `codegen` is a function that generates code, the LLVM intermediate representation (IR). Since the otherwise case is executed last (at compile time), `a` will always be a constant with the value `0`, which you have already observed. gtz = builder.icmp_signed(">", args[0], llty(0)).

Title: recursion with if_else context manager #539 - numba/llvmlite - GitHub
URL: https://github.com/numba/llvmlite/issues/539
Content: In my toy language I have a very simple if-elif .. else construct (like guards in haskell). My method to do this is to recursively nest them.
